title: The Hike

jokes.text: Dobro jutro, my darlings! That, of course, is good morning in... Bosnian. We know!
jokes.setup: Moira dramatically announces foreign phrase and begins to explain
jokes.punchline: Children cut her off - they've heard this pompous behavior before
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira character comedy - her pretentious nature clashing with her children's familiarity with her antics

jokes.text: Okay, have we done something? I'm so confused. What do you want?
jokes.setup: Moira brings breakfast in bed unprompted
jokes.punchline: David's suspicious reaction reveals this kindness is so unusual it seems threatening
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing how dysfunctional family dynamics make kindness suspicious

jokes.text: Aren't these just the cinnamon buns from the lobby? And wouldn't you know it, they have presented themselves unfolded.
jokes.setup: Moira presents 'petite patisserie' breakfast
jokes.punchline: David recognizes them as lobby pastries; Moira's pretentious language about programs being 'unfolded'
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - using flowery language to disguise mundane reality

jokes.text: But then you remember my crippling carpal tunnel from all those years of signing cheques.
jokes.setup: Moira needs help folding programs
jokes.punchline: Her 'disability' is from being wealthy - signing checks, not real work
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific joke showing Moira's complete disconnect from real hardship

jokes.text: Oh, it's just a gesture, David! Stop being so literal.
jokes.setup: David points out coffee has nothing in it
jokes.punchline: Moira dismisses the actual function of coffee as irrelevant detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira logic - the appearance of generosity matters more than actual generosity

jokes.text: Oh my God, Turkish Cosmo once included my photo on their list of the world's best sarongs.
jokes.setup: Ted mentions beaches and being prepared
jokes.punchline: Alexis' ridiculous specific credential for beach expertise
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis - absurdly specific fake accomplishment delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: I don't actually go in the open water, but I can't wait to see you do that.
jokes.setup: Discussion of beach preparation and snorkeling gear
jokes.punchline: Alexis admits she won't use any of it but enjoys watching others
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis' passive approach to adventure

jokes.text: Those are actually an insane safety hazard. If they catch fire, you're basically trapped in a burning cage, so. But, totally.
jokes.setup: Alexis suggests romantic mosquito nets
jokes.punchline: Ted gives horrific safety explanation then casually agrees anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ted character work - scientific precision followed by romantic accommodation

jokes.text: Ew, Ted! What am I, 32?
jokes.setup: Ted compares vaccines to Botox
jokes.punchline: Alexis is offended by implication she's old enough for Botox at 32
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis vanity joke - treating 32 like ancient age for cosmetic procedures

jokes.text: Yeah, I just sorta feel like picnicking by the side of the road is basically an invitation to be murdered.
jokes.setup: Patrick says they've arrived for picnic
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic interpretation of roadside location
jokes.explanation: Classic David anxiety and dramatics about ordinary situations

jokes.text: Yes, so that you can tell me I don't need to!
jokes.setup: Patrick mentions David wanting more physical activity
jokes.punchline: David reveals he only says it to be told he doesn't need to
jokes.explanation: Excellent relationship comedy - David's manipulative approach to self-improvement

jokes.text: David, you have to stop watching 'Notting Hill'. It's not helpful for our relationship.
jokes.setup: David describes romantic picnic expectations
jokes.punchline: Patrick identifies specific rom-com influence on David's unrealistic standards
jokes.explanation: Great relationship dynamic - Patrick knows David well enough to identify his fantasy sources

jokes.text: I could do without the musky aroma though, whew!
jokes.setup: Roland compliments Johnny's 'working-man glow'
jokes.punchline: Immediately undercuts compliment with gross observation
jokes.explanation: Typical Roland tactlessness - giving compliment then ruining it with TMI.

jokes.text: According to Mrs. Rose, our motel is the only place sad enough to pass for a pre-war Berlin brothel.
jokes.setup: Johnny complains about Moira taking furniture
jokes.punchline: The motel's specific qualification for theatrical use
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their living situation - specific historical reference makes it funnier

jokes.text: Just like your youth, right, old man?
jokes.setup: Johnny drops furniture saying it slipped through his fingers
jokes.punchline: Roland's crude age joke
jokes.explanation: Typical Roland humor - obvious and crude but fits his character

jokes.text: You keep everything inside, like a bashful clam!
jokes.setup: Moira says Johnny doesn't communicate his problems
jokes.punchline: Her characteristic flowery metaphor using 'bashful clam'
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira - completely original metaphor that's both absurd and somehow apt

jokes.text: You can try, I don't think you'll have much luck. The poor guy died of a heart attack.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks if they can call Roland's cousin who had same symptoms
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that cousin died from the 'same thing'
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy timing - Roland's obliviousness to how alarming this information is

jokes.text: John? John, can you hear me? Can you hear me? Try to follow the sound of my voice.
jokes.setup: Roland mentions cousin died of heart attack
jokes.punchline: Moira's immediate dramatic overreaction to news about Roland's cousin
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical overreaction is perfectly in character

jokes.text: He predicted when he was going to leave my mom like, to the day.
jokes.setup: Twyla explains how she learned tarot from mom's ex-boyfriend
jokes.punchline: The 'prediction' was just his planned abandonment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered with Twyla's characteristic matter-of-fact tone about family dysfunction

jokes.text: Well, you should probably get your story straight if you're getting people to donate money, though.
jokes.setup: Confusion about whether it's for charity or new dishwasher
jokes.punchline: David's practical concern about fundraising accuracy
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from David being practical about sketchy fundraising

jokes.text: Yeah, so I see a boat capsizing. People screaming. And a couple, like, under water. And then it sort of fades to black.
jokes.setup: Twyla doing tarot reading about trip
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and horrific 'vision' ending with cinematic description
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of doom predictions, 'fades to black' adds movie-like absurdity

jokes.text: Mm, more like a funeral.
jokes.setup: Alexis hopefully asks if the black formal wear is for a party
jokes.punchline: Twyla's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective dark humor - crushing optimistic interpretation

jokes.text: Well, if you can track my eBay bid, then, by all means.
jokes.setup: Patrick says they don't need phone service, they have each other
jokes.punchline: David's sarcastic response about his real priorities
jokes.explanation: Good David character moment - materialistic priorities over romance

jokes.text: Just wonder why we couldn't have found a meadow somewhere, you know, with some men on horseback like, trotting in the background.
jokes.setup: David complaining about hiking to picnic
jokes.punchline: His specific romantic fantasy requiring random horseback riders
jokes.explanation: Perfect David absurdity - hyper-specific romantic fantasy with random elements

jokes.text: Well, the app that's counting my steps isn't working, so I honestly don't know.
jokes.setup: Patrick asks if David wants to turn around
jokes.punchline: David can't make hiking decisions without step-counting app
jokes.explanation: Great David character work - completely dependent on technology for basic decisions

jokes.text: Where's the bear?
jokes.setup: Patrick yells 'ohh!' after stepping on branch
jokes.punchline: David's panicked assumption of worst-case wilderness scenario
jokes.explanation: Good David panic response - immediately assumes nature attack

jokes.text: And I include Caroline in that, and I've had that wig for 40-years.
jokes.setup: Moira declaring Johnny most important thing in world
jokes.punchline: Including her 40-year-old wig named Caroline in the comparison
jokes.explanation: Peak Moira absurdity - treating a named wig as family member in emotional declaration

jokes.text: I'll have to keep the pills, but I'm not one who's the health risk here.
jokes.setup: Moira promising lifestyle changes
jokes.punchline: Immediately making exception for her pills and blaming Johnny
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Moira's inability to take any responsibility

jokes.text: The last time I was in one was with Lenny. Although he didn't stay very long.
jokes.setup: Roland mentions hospitals make him nervous
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to dead cousin again
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke with Roland's continued obliviousness

jokes.text: I once played a nurse on 'M.A.S.H.'
jokes.setup: Doctor using medical code language
jokes.punchline: Moira's completely irrelevant acting credential as medical qualification
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - treating acting role as real expertise in crisis

jokes.text: It was either the first aid kit, or the ice packs.
jokes.setup: David asks which backpack has first aid kit
jokes.punchline: Patrick chose cheese preservation over safety
jokes.explanation: Good priorities humor - romantic gesture over practical safety

jokes.text: and not a Band-aid to be found in either of them, but still.
jokes.setup: David appreciating Patrick's effort packing food
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment noting complete lack of medical supplies
jokes.explanation: Good David delivery - appreciation with pointed criticism

jokes.text: Um, just being on the internet.
jokes.setup: Ted asks what she's doing on computer
jokes.punchline: Casual response while clearly researching boat disasters
jokes.explanation: Good understatement - 'being on internet' while researching death scenarios

jokes.text: Like, I will physically be there, but I will be thinking about them here.
jokes.setup: Alexis struggling to express feeling about missing family
jokes.punchline: Her confusion about the basic concept of missing someone
jokes.explanation: Sweet character development - Alexis discovering family attachment

jokes.text: what you're describing is missing someone, and it is a totally normal feeling.
jokes.setup: Alexis' confused explanation of her feelings
jokes.punchline: Ted's patient explanation of basic human emotion
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Alexis' emotional confusion

jokes.text: What if something happens to David? I'm basically his only friend.
jokes.setup: Alexis worrying about family while away
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment of David's social isolation
jokes.explanation: Dark family humor - Alexis matter-of-factly noting David's friendlessness

jokes.text: I don't do math.
jokes.setup: Stevie worried about running motel alone
jokes.punchline: Simple reason she can't handle business operations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie delivery - blunt honesty about her limitations

jokes.text: And I drained a 20-footer, and finished with an eagle.
jokes.setup: Doctor gives medical update
jokes.punchline: Johnny immediately transitions to golf story
jokes.explanation: Good Johnny character moment - inappropriate golf talk after health scare

jokes.text: Well, he did say that there have been some fatal cases... of heartburn.
jokes.setup: Doctor says Johnny is fine
jokes.punchline: Moira finding way to maintain drama despite good news
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira - refusing to let go of drama even with good news

jokes.text: Yep, I'm just really not looking forward to folding those programs.
jokes.setup: Asked if everything's okay after emotional hospital scene
jokes.punchline: Stevie's mundane concern amid family crisis
jokes.explanation: Good Stevie deflection using callback to opening plot

jokes.text: Plus, you don't want to know what my Mom would do if you showed up to opening night, and couldn't walk.
jokes.setup: David explaining why they stopped hiking
jokes.punchline: Implicit threat about Moira's reaction to Patrick being injured
jokes.explanation: Good family dynamics humor - David knows Moira's priorities

jokes.text: Are these 24-karat? It's a yes, it's a yes! I love you.
jokes.setup: Patrick's romantic proposal speech
jokes.punchline: David's first concern is ring quality before emotional response
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - materialistic concerns before romance, perfectly in character for this pivotal scene

jokes.text: I think the deck may be cursed. I predicted four other drownings today.
jokes.setup: Alexis wants another reading
jokes.punchline: Twyla's matter-of-fact revelation about multiple death predictions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Twyla's bad reading streak delivered deadpan

jokes.text: Either that or it's a stain from a beer bottle. Because my mom's ex gave me the cards.
jokes.setup: Twyla interprets golden ring as prosperity
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that mystical symbol might just be beer stain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Twyla deflation - undermining mystical moment with practical reality

tmdb_overview: Patrick tries to take David on a romantic hike, while Johnny has a health scare that sends everyone into a panic.
insight: Character comedy drives a slower, steadier episode as the Rose family tackles the outdoors.
